title: Method of controlling a printing system
abstract: A method of controlling a printing system that is arranged to process a sequence of media sheets (56a, 56b) and is operable with a finite number of different settings, the method comprising a step of calculating a time to finish (T(b)) that will be needed for processing the sequence, characterized by the steps of scheduling a sequence of sheets (56a, 56b) for printing, defining a set of possible settings for processing the scheduled sequence; calculating a time to finish (T(b)) for each setting in the defined set; selecting the setting with the smallest time to finish; and processing the sequence with the selected setting.
claims:
Claims 1. A method of controlling a printing system that is arranged to process a sequence of media sheets (56a, 56b) and is operable with a finite number of different settings, the method comprising a step of calculating a time to finish (T(b)) that will be needed for processing the sequence, characterized by the steps of a) scheduling a sequence of sheets (56a, 56b) for printing, b) defining a set of possible settings for processing the scheduled sequence; c) calculating a time to finish (T(b)) for each setting in the defined set; d) selecting the setting with the smallest time to finish; and e) processing the sequence with the selected setting. 2. The method according to claim 1 , wherein each setting is designated by a vector with a finite number of components, each component of the vector corresponding to a setting parameter of the printing system. 3. The method according to claim 2, wherein one setting parameter is a fuse temperature or printing speed. 4. The method according to claim 2 or 3, wherein one setting parameter is a parameter specifying duplex or simplex printing. 5. The method according to any of the claims 2 to 4, wherein one setting parameter specifies a colour mode of the printing system. 6. The method according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the step (a) comprises scheduling a number (b) of sheets that corresponds to only a part of a print job. 7. A method according to any of the claims 1 to 5, wherein the step (a) comprises scheduling a number (b) of sheets that contains all the sheets of at least one entire print job. 8. The method according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the step (e) comprises processing selected ones of the sheets in the sequence with a setting 

 different from the setting selected in step (d), in accordance with given printing instructions for these sheets. 9. The method according to any of the preceding claims, comprising a step of dividing the sequence scheduled in step (a) into sub-sequences and repeating the steps (b) to (e) for each sub-sequence, and a step (S15) of deciding whether to keep the setting that has been selected for the entire sequence or select different settings for each sub-sequence. 10. The method according to any of the preceding claims, comprising a step of dividing the sequence scheduled in step (a) into sub-sequences, wherein one of the settings of step (b) comprises the order in which to process the sub-sequences in step (e), and wherein step (e) further comprises processing the sequence with the selected ordering setting. 1 1 . A printing system that arranged to process a sequence of media sheets (56a, 56b) and operable with a finite number of different settings, the comprising a controller (22) capable of calculating a time to finish (T(b)) that will be needed for processing the sequence, characterized in that the controller is configured to perform the method according to any of the claims 1 to 10. 12. A computer program product comprising program code on a computer-readable medium, which program code, when executed on a controller (22) of a printing system, causes the controller to perform the method according to any of the claims 1 to 10.
